title: CodecWireFormat
description: Fixed-width alphanumeric wire format for transmitting building records over low-bandwidth radio (AFSK/codec path). Every field has a fixed character width so decode is unambiguous without delimiters. Coordinates are stored as signed integers (degrees × 100000) to preserve sign — never use Math.abs on lat or lon.

_wireFormat.description: Total encoded string layout (all fields concatenated, no separators). Total = 32 characters.

fields.name: checksum
fields.encoding: sum of all preceding character codes mod 65536, zero-padded 4-digit hex

buildingRecord.description: In-memory representation before encoding / after decoding.

lat.description: WGS-84 latitude, signed float degrees. Negative = south. NEVER store as abs.

lon.description: WGS-84 longitude, signed float degrees. Negative = west. NEVER store as abs.

anomaly.description: True if an operator has manually flagged a crowdsourced anomaly via the in-app form. Never set from external API.

_coordinateTolerance: ±0.00001 degrees (one unit of the ×100000 integer quantization) is acceptable round-trip error for lat/lon. id, status, anomaly must be bit-exact.
_prohibitedPatterns: Math.abs(lat) or Math.abs(lon) anywhere in encode or decode path
Variable-length lat/lon fields (always use fixed widths as specified above)
Decode that only reads status and ignores coordinates
